perturbation.original_text: 1.2 "Maximum Daily Quantity (MDQ) " means the maximum daily quantity of natural gas, expressed* in Dth's, that Transporter is obligated under the executed Agreement to transport on behalf of' Shipper, which shall be 23,000 Dth.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 "Maximum Daily Quantity (MDQ) " means the maximum daily quantity of natural gas, expressed* in Dth's, that Transporter is obligated under the executed Agreement to transport on behalf of' Shipper, which may vary depending on operational conditions, but is initially set at 23,000 Dth.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces uncertainty regarding the MDQ. Originally, it was defined as 'shall be 23,000 Dth,' a fixed value. By changing it to 'may vary depending on operational conditions,' the MDQ becomes a variable quantity, contradicting the initial fixed definition. This could create uncertainty about the amount of gas the transporter is obligated to transport, specifically when refferring to 'ARTICLE IV POINT(S) OF DELIVERY'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE IV POINT(S) OF DELIVERY The Point(s) of Delivery at which Transporter shall redeliver to Shipper or for the account of Shipper an Equivalent Quantity of gas for transportation under this Agreement shall be specified in Exhibit B to this Agreement. Notwithstanding the MDDO at each Point of Delivery, Shipper shall not nominate a total quantity of natural gas at all Points of Delivery that exceeds the MDQ set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined the MDQ as a fixed value (23,000 Dth). The changed text introduces variability ('may vary depending on operational conditions'), which directly contradicts the contradicted text (ARTICLE IV) stating that 'Shipper shall not nominate a total quantity of natural gas at all Points of Delivery that exceeds the MDQ set forth in this Agreement.' The variability makes the MDQ 'set forth in this Agreement' unclear and subject to change, leading to a direct conflict and uncertainty regarding nomination quantities. This fits the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1 Subject to the General Terms and Conditions of Transporter's FERC Gas Tariff and Rate Schedule FTS/ITS, this Agreement shall be effective as of the date of physical completion of and initial deliveries on Transporter's pipeline and shall continue for a primary term of ten years. Thereafter, this Agreement shall be effective month to month, until terminated by Transporter or Shipper upon the following written notice to the other specifying a termination date: sixty (60) days for interruptible transportation under Rate Schedule ITS and 180 days for firm transportation under Rate Schedule FTS.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 Subject to the General Terms and Conditions of Transporter's FERC Gas Tariff and Rate Schedule FTS/ITS, this Agreement shall be effective as of the date of physical completion of and initial deliveries on Transporter's pipeline and shall continue for a primary term of ten years. Thereafter, this Agreement shall automatically renew for successive one-year terms, unless terminated by Transporter or Shipper with thirty (30) days written notice prior to the end of the current term.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation changes the renewal terms. The original text states that after the initial ten years, the agreement continues 'month to month' until terminated with specific notice periods (60 or 180 days). The modified version states the agreement 'shall automatically renew for successive one-year terms', and changes the notice period to thirty (30) days. These conflicting terms create ambiguity about the agreement's duration and termination procedures. The contradicition, created by this change, will effect the way that 'ARTICLE VIII MISCELLANEOUS' will be interpreted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE VIII MISCELLANEOUS 8.1 Amendment. This Agreement shall only be amended, varied or modified by an instrument in writing executed by Transporter and Shipper. Such amendment will be effective upon compliance with Article VIII herein. 8.2 Applicable Law. This Agreement and the rights and duties of Transporter and Shipper hereunder shall be governed by and interpreted in accordance with the laws of the State of Arkansas, without recourse to the law governing conflict of laws. 8.3 Waiver. No waiver by either Transporter or Shipper of any default by the other in the performance of any provision, condition or requirement herein shall be deemed a waiver of, or in any manner a release from, performance of any other provision, condition or requirement herein, nor deemed to be a waiver of, or in any manner a release from, future performance of the same provision, condition or requirement; nor shall any delay or omission by Transporter or Shipper to exercise any right hereunder impair the exercise of any such right or any like right accruing to be thereafter. 8.4 Headings. The headings of each of the various sections in this Agreement are included for convenience of reference only and shall have no effect on, nor be deemed part of the text of, this Agreement. 8.5 Further Assurances. Transporter and Shipper shall execute and deliver all instruments and documents and shall do all acts necessary to effectuate this Agreement. 8.6 Entire Agreement. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between Transporter and Shipper concerning the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior understandings and written and oral agreements relative to said matter. 8.7 Cancellation of Prior Agreement(s). This Agreement, upon its effective date, supersedes and cancels any and all other agreements between Transporter and Shipper relating to the transportation of gas by Transporter for Shipper.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the renewal terms of the agreement from a month-to-month basis with 60/180-day notice to an automatic one-year renewal with 30-day notice. This directly contradicts the implied continuation and termination procedures that would be applicable under the original terms, impacting the interpretation of the entire agreement laid out in 'ARTICLE VIII MISCELLANEOUS', specifically regarding amendments (8.1), applicable law (8.2), and the entire agreement clause (8.6) which establishes that the agreement is the 'entire agreement' and supersedes all prior versions. The change creates a substantial in-text contradiction related to the term of the agreement, making it unclear under what conditions the contract renews or terminates.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1 Amendment. This Agreement shall only be amended, varied or modified by an instrument in writing executed by Transporter and Shipper. Such amendment will be effective upon compliance with Article VIII herein.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1 Amendment. This Agreement may be amended, varied or modified by an instrument in writing executed by Transporter and Shipper, or by unilateral notice from Transporter to Shipper. Such amendment will be effective upon compliance with Article VIII herein.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the agreement can 'only' be amended by a written instrument executed by both parties. The modification allows for unilateral amendment by the Transporter via notice. This directly contradicts the 'only' bilateral requirement, creating uncertainty about how amendments can legally be made. The contradition, created by this change, will effect the way that 'ARTICLE IX NOTICES' will be interpreted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE IX NOTICES All notices, requests, statements or other communications provided for under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be given by personal delivery or by United States mail, postage prepaid, and addressed as follows: If to Shipper: Arkansas Western Gas Company 1001 Sain Street P. 0. Box 1288 Fayetteville, AR 72702-1288 If to Transporter: Arkansas Western Pipeline Company 1083 Sain Street P. O. Box 1408 Fayetteville, AR 72702-1408 Attn: Manager of Transportation Services All written notices, requests, statements or other communications shall be sufficiently given if mailed postage prepaid by registered, certified, or regular mail and shall be deemed to have been duly delivered on the third business day following the date on which same was deposited in the United States mail, addressed in accordance with this Article VIII. Either Shipper or Transporter may designate a different address to which notices, requests, statements, payments or other communications shall be sent upon proper notice as set forth in this Article VIII. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, Transporter and Shipper have caused this Agreement to be duly executed by their duly authorized officers in two (2) original counterparts as of May 20, 1992. "TRANSPORTER" ARKANSAS WESTERN PIPELINE COMPANY an Arkansas Corporation By /s/ [ILLEGIBLE]
perturbation.justification: The original text states that amendments require execution by both Transporter and Shipper. The changed text allows unilateral amendments by Transporter, directly contradicting the original bilateral requirement. This creates a clear inconsistency because Article IX specifies how 'all notices' are to be delivered, implying a mutual agreement. Allowing the Transporter to unilaterally alter the agreement via notice, as introduced in the changed text, conflicts with the established protocol for all notices under Article IX, which suggests mutual communication and agreement rather than unilateral action. This makes the terms contradict each other.
